commits.author: Hz_Zhang
commits.message:
Add blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS to avoid busy-polling the lookup wait (#3704)

* Add blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS to avoid busy-polling the lookup wait

The SGLang MP adapter's _wait_for_lookup busy-polled QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS in
a 1ms sleep loop, adding repeated ZMQ requests and polling latency on the load
path -- noticeable when a lookup triggers movement from a slower storage layer
into L1 (per the TODO left in _wait_for_lookup).

Add a blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS request type. Its daemon handler blocks on
a PrefetchController completion condition variable until the prefetch result is
published (or a timeout), so the client issues a single request instead of
busy-polling. L1-only prefetches (prefetch_request_id == -1) return immediately.

Fixes #3694

Signed-off-by: Haozhe Zhang <zhang.hz6666@gmail.com>

* Register WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS in the engine protocol definitions

The RequestType docstring requires every enum member to also have a protocol
definition (the validation system in protocols/__init__.py enforces a 1:1
match). The new WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS member was missing one, so
initialize_protocols() would fail with an orphaned enum member. Add the
definition (payload: request_id str + timeout float, returns int | None) and
its REQUEST_NAMES entry, mirroring QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS.

Signed-off-by: Haozhe Zhang <zhang.hz6666@gmail.com>

* test: cover the WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS LookupModule handler path

Add targeted tests for wait_prefetch_status -> query_prefetch_status: count
computation + event emission + exactly-once job consumption on success, None +
job retention on timeout, and 0 for an unknown request_id. The storage manager is
mocked, so the request-handler/protocol path is exercised without a GPU or native
bitmap (the controller tests already cover the condition variable in isolation).

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

* style: ruff-format the WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS test

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

* docs: document WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS in the MP ZMQ protocol (SGLang-only)

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

feat(mp): introduce device-agnostic DeviceIPCWrapper base for KV-cache IPC (#3703)

All KV-cache IPC wrappers (CUDA, CPU-SHM, TRT-LLM raw-CUDA) previously
subclassed `CudaIPCWrapper` — a naming and structural pattern that made
non-CUDA backends impossible to add cleanly.  This change introduces a
device-agnostic base class and reparents the existing wrappers onto it.

    :dtype, shape, stride, storage_offset, device_uuid.
  - `CudaIPCWrapper` reparented to `DeviceIPCWrapper`.
  - `RawCudaIPCWrapper` reparented to `DeviceIPCWrapper`.
  - Update `list[CudaIPCWrapper]` to `list[DeviceIPCWrapper]`.
  - `_CUSTOMERIZED_SERIALIZERS` ext-code-1 key updated to
    `DeviceIPCWrapper`; pickle still preserves concrete subclass identity
    through the wire so `to_tensor()` dispatches correctly.

lmcache/v1/platform/cpu/shm.py
  - `CpuShmTensorWrapper` reparented to `DeviceIPCWrapper`.

lmcache/v1/multiprocess/mq.py
  - Import and `_SPECIAL_ENCODER_DECODERS` keys updated:
    `CudaIPCWrapper` / `list[CudaIPCWrapper]` -> `DeviceIPCWrapper` /
    `list[DeviceIPCWrapper]`.

lmcache/v1/multiprocess/protocols/blend_v3.py
lmcache/v1/multiprocess/modules/blend_v3.py
  - Import and payload-class / type-annotation references updated to
    `DeviceIPCWrapper`.

commits.message:
[Feat]: HiddenStateStore for hidden-state caching (#3221)

* Restore hs_bridge and config edits after submodule migration

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* feat(v1): HiddenStateStore and engine wiring for hidden-state caching

- Add HiddenStateStore (chunk-aligned CPU pool, lazy KV-coupled eviction)
- Wire LMCacheEngine.hidden_state_store when enable_hidden_state_cache
- Add config keys: enable_hidden_state_cache, max_hidden_state_cpu_size,
  hidden_state_layers
- Tests and design doc for the store API

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* fix(v1): apply PR #3221 review suggestions to HiddenStateStore

- Move private helpers (_chunk, _kv_present, _alloc_chunk, _evict_one_lru,
  _free_key) to end of class per repo style guide
- Replace magic retry count (8) with _HS_ALLOC_MAX_RETRIES module constant
- Replace assert tensor is not None with explicit RuntimeError in store and
  retrieve paths; release obj on failure in store path to prevent memory leak
- Drop redundant tensor.clone() in retrieve_hidden_states (torch.cat already
  produces a new contiguous tensor)
- Add token_offset kwarg to store_hidden_states (default 0, backward-compat):
  callers pass full token_ids for correct KV-aligned chunk keys while
  providing only token_ids[token_offset:] as hidden_states, enabling
  incremental store without zero-padding (Vivo50E suggestion)
- Add tests: test_store_with_token_offset_aligns_with_kv_keys,
  test_store_token_offset_rejects_bad_args

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* fix(v1): address PR #3221 review feedback for HiddenStateStore

Avoid materializing a full CPU/float32 copy on store by copying per-chunk
slices directly into pinned MemoryObj buffers. Introduce MemoryFormat.HS_TD
so hidden-state allocations are tagged separately from encoder cache. On
retrieve, refresh LRU in reverse prefix order so suffix chunks are evicted
before prefix chunks under pool pressure. Expand design and user docs, and
add tests covering reverse LRU ordering and eviction behavior.

Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* Restore encoder-cache config dropped in dev merge; lint hidden-state changes

The previous merge of dev into this branch regressed lmcache/v1/config.py:
it kept the branch's stale config.py and dropped dev's additions, including
load_ec_engine_config (still imported by lmcache/integration/vllm/utils.py
and tests/v1/test_config.py), causing an ImportError that failed unit-test
collection. Rebuild config.py from dev and re-apply only the hidden-state
config keys. Also apply isort/ruff-format to the hidden-state files and fix
two E501 lines flagged by ruff.

Signed-off-by: Samuel Shen <slshen@tensormesh.ai>

* Drop unused HS bridge; fix HiddenStateStore mypy errors

hs_bridge.py (LMCacheHSBridge) called LMCacheEngine.store_hidden_state_chunks
and .retrieve_hidden_states, which never existed on the engine (the real API
lives on engine.hidden_state_store). The module was unused and untested inside
LMCache, so remove it rather than ship a broken helper; drop its mention from
the hidden_state_layers config docstring.

Also fix the two remaining mypy errors: cast _chunk()'s process_tokens result
to the CacheEngineKey-keyed form HiddenStateStore relies on, and ignore the
arg-type on the test fake passed to bind_storage_manager.

Signed-off-by: Samuel Shen <slshen@tensormesh.ai>

commits.author: Omer Rubinstein
commits.message:
feat(valkey): add per-key TTL + partial-chunk validation to Valkey glide connector (#3836)

* feat(valkey): reject partial/unfull chunks in Valkey glide connector

The Valkey GLIDE connector uses single-key, fixed-size storage with no
per-chunk metadata (same scheme as RESPConnector), so partial/unfull
chunks cannot be represented or verified on read. Validate this in
ValkeyConnectorAdapter by asserting save_chunk_meta and save_unfull_chunk
are both False, mirroring RESPConnectorAdapter.

Also coerce free-form extra_config booleans via _to_bool so a string
like "false" is parsed correctly (bool("false") is truthy); applied to
save_chunk_meta and the existing tls_enable read.

Additionally, harden _do_get_into so a short read is not silently
accepted: it previously returned True regardless of bytes read, leaving
stale tail bytes in the destination buffer and masking a size mismatch /
corruption. Warn and treat a size mismatch as a miss (return False) on
both the buffer-GET and legacy GET paths so the entry is recomputed.
Fixed-size KV chunks must round-trip exactly.

Tests cover the real _do_get_into buffer-GET return path directly, and
the in-memory mock now faithfully models the buffer-GET branch (returning
an int byte count) so get/batched_get are exercised on both GET paths.

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

* feat(valkey): add optional per-key TTL to Valkey glide connector

Without a TTL, keys are persisted indefinitely, so a Valkey/Redis node
using a volatile-lru/volatile-lfu eviction policy never evicts the L2
KV cache keys and chokes once it reaches maxmemory. Add an opt-in,
configurable per-key TTL, gated behind a feature flag, so the eviction
policy can reclaim memory under pressure.

New extra_config keys:
- valkey_enable_ttl (bool, default false): feature flag.
- valkey_ttl_sec (int seconds, default 86400 when enabled but unset):
  per-key TTL; must be a positive integer.

_ThreadWorkerPool and ValkeyConnector accept ttl_seconds and, when set,
write keys with glide_sync.ExpirySet(SEC, ttl) (built lazily and cached;
the immutable ExpirySet is safe to share across worker threads). The
no-TTL path is unchanged. Adds docs and unit tests covering config
passthrough, defaulting, invalid-value rejection, and expiry application
on put.

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

commits.author: nayeonikim
commits.message:
[Fix][RawBlock] Roll back put-task refs when dispatch scheduling fails (#3698)

batched_submit_put_task raises a ref on each pending object and reserves
its key in _put_tasks before handing the batch to the event loop. If
asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe fails (e.g. the loop is shutting down),
the raised refs and reserved keys for the unscheduled items leaked,
keeping the MemoryObj pinned and the key permanently blocked from future
puts. Wrap dispatch so the unscheduled items roll back their ref and
put-task entry; already-scheduled items still release theirs in the
coroutine's finally block.

Signed-off-by: Nayeon Kim <nayeoni.kim@samsung.com>

commits.author: nafis271
commits.message:
[GPU connector] Allow non-zero storage_offset in dim-0-padded layout (MTP + CPU offload) (#3853)

* [GPU connector] Allow non-zero storage_offset in dim-0-padded layout

The storage_offset == 0 constraint in _validate_dim0_padded_layout (added
in #3171, intentionally narrow) rejects MTP speculative decoding together
with LMCache CPU offload: with MTP on, per-layer KV are slice/narrow views
(storage_offset != 0). The offset is carried through on the GPU path --
CudaIPCWrapper round-trips storage_offset and get_group_data_ptrs passes
tensor.data_ptr() (offset baked in) to the transfer kernel, so
block_stride_elems is applied from the correct base. The CPU/SHM tier keeps
its own offset==0 assert.

Fixes #3821

Signed-off-by: Md Nafis Ul Haque Shifat <nafis@deepinfra.com>

feat(mp): introduce device-agnostic DeviceIPCWrapper base for KV-cache IPC (#3703)

All KV-cache IPC wrappers (CUDA, CPU-SHM, TRT-LLM raw-CUDA) previously
subclassed `CudaIPCWrapper` — a naming and structural pattern that made
non-CUDA backends impossible to add cleanly.  This change introduces a
device-agnostic base class and reparents the existing wrappers onto it.

    :dtype, shape, stride, storage_offset, device_uuid.
  - `CudaIPCWrapper` reparented to `DeviceIPCWrapper`.
  - `RawCudaIPCWrapper` reparented to `DeviceIPCWrapper`.
  - Update `list[CudaIPCWrapper]` to `list[DeviceIPCWrapper]`.
  - `_CUSTOMERIZED_SERIALIZERS` ext-code-1 key updated to
    `DeviceIPCWrapper`; pickle still preserves concrete subclass identity
    through the wire so `to_tensor()` dispatches correctly.

lmcache/v1/platform/cpu/shm.py
  - `CpuShmTensorWrapper` reparented to `DeviceIPCWrapper`.

lmcache/v1/multiprocess/mq.py
  - Import and `_SPECIAL_ENCODER_DECODERS` keys updated:
    `CudaIPCWrapper` / `list[CudaIPCWrapper]` -> `DeviceIPCWrapper` /
    `list[DeviceIPCWrapper]`.

lmcache/v1/multiprocess/protocols/blend_v3.py
lmcache/v1/multiprocess/modules/blend_v3.py
  - Import and payload-class / type-annotation references updated to
    `DeviceIPCWrapper`.

commits.author: Hz_Zhang
commits.message:
Add blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS to avoid busy-polling the lookup wait (#3704)

* Add blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS to avoid busy-polling the lookup wait

The SGLang MP adapter's _wait_for_lookup busy-polled QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS in
a 1ms sleep loop, adding repeated ZMQ requests and polling latency on the load
path -- noticeable when a lookup triggers movement from a slower storage layer
into L1 (per the TODO left in _wait_for_lookup).

Add a blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS request type. Its daemon handler blocks on
a PrefetchController completion condition variable until the prefetch result is
published (or a timeout), so the client issues a single request instead of
busy-polling. L1-only prefetches (prefetch_request_id == -1) return immediately.

Fixes #3694

Signed-off-by: Haozhe Zhang <zhang.hz6666@gmail.com>

* Register WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS in the engine protocol definitions

The RequestType docstring requires every enum member to also have a protocol
definition (the validation system in protocols/__init__.py enforces a 1:1
match). The new WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS member was missing one, so
initialize_protocols() would fail with an orphaned enum member. Add the
definition (payload: request_id str + timeout float, returns int | None) and
its REQUEST_NAMES entry, mirroring QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS.

Signed-off-by: Haozhe Zhang <zhang.hz6666@gmail.com>

* test: cover the WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS LookupModule handler path

Add targeted tests for wait_prefetch_status -> query_prefetch_status: count
computation + event emission + exactly-once job consumption on success, None +
job retention on timeout, and 0 for an unknown request_id. The storage manager is
mocked, so the request-handler/protocol path is exercised without a GPU or native
bitmap (the controller tests already cover the condition variable in isolation).

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

* style: ruff-format the WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS test

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

* docs: document WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS in the MP ZMQ protocol (SGLang-only)

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

commits.message:
[Feat]: HiddenStateStore for hidden-state caching (#3221)

* Restore hs_bridge and config edits after submodule migration

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* feat(v1): HiddenStateStore and engine wiring for hidden-state caching

- Add HiddenStateStore (chunk-aligned CPU pool, lazy KV-coupled eviction)
- Wire LMCacheEngine.hidden_state_store when enable_hidden_state_cache
- Add config keys: enable_hidden_state_cache, max_hidden_state_cpu_size,
  hidden_state_layers
- Tests and design doc for the store API

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* fix(v1): apply PR #3221 review suggestions to HiddenStateStore

- Move private helpers (_chunk, _kv_present, _alloc_chunk, _evict_one_lru,
  _free_key) to end of class per repo style guide
- Replace magic retry count (8) with _HS_ALLOC_MAX_RETRIES module constant
- Replace assert tensor is not None with explicit RuntimeError in store and
  retrieve paths; release obj on failure in store path to prevent memory leak
- Drop redundant tensor.clone() in retrieve_hidden_states (torch.cat already
  produces a new contiguous tensor)
- Add token_offset kwarg to store_hidden_states (default 0, backward-compat):
  callers pass full token_ids for correct KV-aligned chunk keys while
  providing only token_ids[token_offset:] as hidden_states, enabling
  incremental store without zero-padding (Vivo50E suggestion)
- Add tests: test_store_with_token_offset_aligns_with_kv_keys,
  test_store_token_offset_rejects_bad_args

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* fix(v1): address PR #3221 review feedback for HiddenStateStore

Avoid materializing a full CPU/float32 copy on store by copying per-chunk
slices directly into pinned MemoryObj buffers. Introduce MemoryFormat.HS_TD
so hidden-state allocations are tagged separately from encoder cache. On
retrieve, refresh LRU in reverse prefix order so suffix chunks are evicted
before prefix chunks under pool pressure. Expand design and user docs, and
add tests covering reverse LRU ordering and eviction behavior.

Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* Restore encoder-cache config dropped in dev merge; lint hidden-state changes

The previous merge of dev into this branch regressed lmcache/v1/config.py:
it kept the branch's stale config.py and dropped dev's additions, including
load_ec_engine_config (still imported by lmcache/integration/vllm/utils.py
and tests/v1/test_config.py), causing an ImportError that failed unit-test
collection. Rebuild config.py from dev and re-apply only the hidden-state
config keys. Also apply isort/ruff-format to the hidden-state files and fix
two E501 lines flagged by ruff.

Signed-off-by: Samuel Shen <slshen@tensormesh.ai>

* Drop unused HS bridge; fix HiddenStateStore mypy errors

hs_bridge.py (LMCacheHSBridge) called LMCacheEngine.store_hidden_state_chunks
and .retrieve_hidden_states, which never existed on the engine (the real API
lives on engine.hidden_state_store). The module was unused and untested inside
LMCache, so remove it rather than ship a broken helper; drop its mention from
the hidden_state_layers config docstring.

Also fix the two remaining mypy errors: cast _chunk()'s process_tokens result
to the CacheEngineKey-keyed form HiddenStateStore relies on, and ignore the
arg-type on the test fake passed to bind_storage_manager.

Signed-off-by: Samuel Shen <slshen@tensormesh.ai>

commits.author: Omer Rubinstein
commits.message:
feat(valkey): add per-key TTL + partial-chunk validation to Valkey glide connector (#3836)

* feat(valkey): reject partial/unfull chunks in Valkey glide connector

The Valkey GLIDE connector uses single-key, fixed-size storage with no
per-chunk metadata (same scheme as RESPConnector), so partial/unfull
chunks cannot be represented or verified on read. Validate this in
ValkeyConnectorAdapter by asserting save_chunk_meta and save_unfull_chunk
are both False, mirroring RESPConnectorAdapter.

Also coerce free-form extra_config booleans via _to_bool so a string
like "false" is parsed correctly (bool("false") is truthy); applied to
save_chunk_meta and the existing tls_enable read.

Additionally, harden _do_get_into so a short read is not silently
accepted: it previously returned True regardless of bytes read, leaving
stale tail bytes in the destination buffer and masking a size mismatch /
corruption. Warn and treat a size mismatch as a miss (return False) on
both the buffer-GET and legacy GET paths so the entry is recomputed.
Fixed-size KV chunks must round-trip exactly.

Tests cover the real _do_get_into buffer-GET return path directly, and
the in-memory mock now faithfully models the buffer-GET branch (returning
an int byte count) so get/batched_get are exercised on both GET paths.

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

* feat(valkey): add optional per-key TTL to Valkey glide connector

Without a TTL, keys are persisted indefinitely, so a Valkey/Redis node
using a volatile-lru/volatile-lfu eviction policy never evicts the L2
KV cache keys and chokes once it reaches maxmemory. Add an opt-in,
configurable per-key TTL, gated behind a feature flag, so the eviction
policy can reclaim memory under pressure.

New extra_config keys:
- valkey_enable_ttl (bool, default false): feature flag.
- valkey_ttl_sec (int seconds, default 86400 when enabled but unset):
  per-key TTL; must be a positive integer.

_ThreadWorkerPool and ValkeyConnector accept ttl_seconds and, when set,
write keys with glide_sync.ExpirySet(SEC, ttl) (built lazily and cached;
the immutable ExpirySet is safe to share across worker threads). The
no-TTL path is unchanged. Adds docs and unit tests covering config
passthrough, defaulting, invalid-value rejection, and expiry application
on put.

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

commits.author: nayeonikim
commits.message:
[Fix][RawBlock] Roll back put-task refs when dispatch scheduling fails (#3698)

batched_submit_put_task raises a ref on each pending object and reserves
its key in _put_tasks before handing the batch to the event loop. If
asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe fails (e.g. the loop is shutting down),
the raised refs and reserved keys for the unscheduled items leaked,
keeping the MemoryObj pinned and the key permanently blocked from future
puts. Wrap dispatch so the unscheduled items roll back their ref and
put-task entry; already-scheduled items still release theirs in the
coroutine's finally block.

Signed-off-by: Nayeon Kim <nayeoni.kim@samsung.com>

commits.message:
[GPU connector] Allow non-zero storage_offset in dim-0-padded layout (MTP + CPU offload) (#3853)

* [GPU connector] Allow non-zero storage_offset in dim-0-padded layout

The storage_offset == 0 constraint in _validate_dim0_padded_layout (added
in #3171, intentionally narrow) rejects MTP speculative decoding together
with LMCache CPU offload: with MTP on, per-layer KV are slice/narrow views
(storage_offset != 0). The offset is carried through on the GPU path --
CudaIPCWrapper round-trips storage_offset and get_group_data_ptrs passes
tensor.data_ptr() (offset baked in) to the transfer kernel, so
block_stride_elems is applied from the correct base. The CPU/SHM tier keeps
its own offset==0 assert.

Fixes #3821

commits.author: Hz_Zhang
commits.message:
Add blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS to avoid busy-polling the lookup wait (#3704)

* Add blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS to avoid busy-polling the lookup wait

The SGLang MP adapter's _wait_for_lookup busy-polled QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS in
a 1ms sleep loop, adding repeated ZMQ requests and polling latency on the load
path -- noticeable when a lookup triggers movement from a slower storage layer
into L1 (per the TODO left in _wait_for_lookup).

Add a blocking WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS request type. Its daemon handler blocks on
a PrefetchController completion condition variable until the prefetch result is
published (or a timeout), so the client issues a single request instead of
busy-polling. L1-only prefetches (prefetch_request_id == -1) return immediately.

Fixes #3694

Signed-off-by: Haozhe Zhang <zhang.hz6666@gmail.com>

* Register WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS in the engine protocol definitions

The RequestType docstring requires every enum member to also have a protocol
definition (the validation system in protocols/__init__.py enforces a 1:1
match). The new WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS member was missing one, so
initialize_protocols() would fail with an orphaned enum member. Add the
definition (payload: request_id str + timeout float, returns int | None) and
its REQUEST_NAMES entry, mirroring QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS.

Signed-off-by: Haozhe Zhang <zhang.hz6666@gmail.com>

* test: cover the WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS LookupModule handler path

Add targeted tests for wait_prefetch_status -> query_prefetch_status: count
computation + event emission + exactly-once job consumption on success, None +
job retention on timeout, and 0 for an unknown request_id. The storage manager is
mocked, so the request-handler/protocol path is exercised without a GPU or native
bitmap (the controller tests already cover the condition variable in isolation).

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

* style: ruff-format the WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS test

Signed-off-by: haozhe <haozhe@haozhes-MacBook-Pro.local>

* docs: document WAIT_PREFETCH_STATUS in the MP ZMQ protocol (SGLang-only)

commits.message:
[Feat]: HiddenStateStore for hidden-state caching (#3221)

* Restore hs_bridge and config edits after submodule migration

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* feat(v1): HiddenStateStore and engine wiring for hidden-state caching

- Add HiddenStateStore (chunk-aligned CPU pool, lazy KV-coupled eviction)
- Wire LMCacheEngine.hidden_state_store when enable_hidden_state_cache
- Add config keys: enable_hidden_state_cache, max_hidden_state_cpu_size,
  hidden_state_layers
- Tests and design doc for the store API

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* fix(v1): apply PR #3221 review suggestions to HiddenStateStore

- Move private helpers (_chunk, _kv_present, _alloc_chunk, _evict_one_lru,
  _free_key) to end of class per repo style guide
- Replace magic retry count (8) with _HS_ALLOC_MAX_RETRIES module constant
- Replace assert tensor is not None with explicit RuntimeError in store and
  retrieve paths; release obj on failure in store path to prevent memory leak
- Drop redundant tensor.clone() in retrieve_hidden_states (torch.cat already
  produces a new contiguous tensor)
- Add token_offset kwarg to store_hidden_states (default 0, backward-compat):
  callers pass full token_ids for correct KV-aligned chunk keys while
  providing only token_ids[token_offset:] as hidden_states, enabling
  incremental store without zero-padding (Vivo50E suggestion)
- Add tests: test_store_with_token_offset_aligns_with_kv_keys,
  test_store_token_offset_rejects_bad_args

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* fix(v1): address PR #3221 review feedback for HiddenStateStore

Avoid materializing a full CPU/float32 copy on store by copying per-chunk
slices directly into pinned MemoryObj buffers. Introduce MemoryFormat.HS_TD
so hidden-state allocations are tagged separately from encoder cache. On
retrieve, refresh LRU in reverse prefix order so suffix chunks are evicted
before prefix chunks under pool pressure. Expand design and user docs, and
add tests covering reverse LRU ordering and eviction behavior.

Signed-off-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@g297.voltagepark.net>

* Restore encoder-cache config dropped in dev merge; lint hidden-state changes

The previous merge of dev into this branch regressed lmcache/v1/config.py:
it kept the branch's stale config.py and dropped dev's additions, including
load_ec_engine_config (still imported by lmcache/integration/vllm/utils.py
and tests/v1/test_config.py), causing an ImportError that failed unit-test
collection. Rebuild config.py from dev and re-apply only the hidden-state
config keys. Also apply isort/ruff-format to the hidden-state files and fix
two E501 lines flagged by ruff.

Signed-off-by: Samuel Shen <slshen@tensormesh.ai>

* Drop unused HS bridge; fix HiddenStateStore mypy errors

hs_bridge.py (LMCacheHSBridge) called LMCacheEngine.store_hidden_state_chunks
and .retrieve_hidden_states, which never existed on the engine (the real API
lives on engine.hidden_state_store). The module was unused and untested inside
LMCache, so remove it rather than ship a broken helper; drop its mention from
the hidden_state_layers config docstring.

Also fix the two remaining mypy errors: cast _chunk()'s process_tokens result
to the CacheEngineKey-keyed form HiddenStateStore relies on, and ignore the
arg-type on the test fake passed to bind_storage_manager.

Signed-off-by: Samuel Shen <slshen@tensormesh.ai>

commits.author: Omer Rubinstein
commits.message:
feat(valkey): add per-key TTL + partial-chunk validation to Valkey glide connector (#3836)

* feat(valkey): reject partial/unfull chunks in Valkey glide connector

The Valkey GLIDE connector uses single-key, fixed-size storage with no
per-chunk metadata (same scheme as RESPConnector), so partial/unfull
chunks cannot be represented or verified on read. Validate this in
ValkeyConnectorAdapter by asserting save_chunk_meta and save_unfull_chunk
are both False, mirroring RESPConnectorAdapter.

Also coerce free-form extra_config booleans via _to_bool so a string
like "false" is parsed correctly (bool("false") is truthy); applied to
save_chunk_meta and the existing tls_enable read.

Additionally, harden _do_get_into so a short read is not silently
accepted: it previously returned True regardless of bytes read, leaving
stale tail bytes in the destination buffer and masking a size mismatch /
corruption. Warn and treat a size mismatch as a miss (return False) on
both the buffer-GET and legacy GET paths so the entry is recomputed.
Fixed-size KV chunks must round-trip exactly.

Tests cover the real _do_get_into buffer-GET return path directly, and
the in-memory mock now faithfully models the buffer-GET branch (returning
an int byte count) so get/batched_get are exercised on both GET paths.

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

* feat(valkey): add optional per-key TTL to Valkey glide connector

Without a TTL, keys are persisted indefinitely, so a Valkey/Redis node
using a volatile-lru/volatile-lfu eviction policy never evicts the L2
KV cache keys and chokes once it reaches maxmemory. Add an opt-in,
configurable per-key TTL, gated behind a feature flag, so the eviction
policy can reclaim memory under pressure.

New extra_config keys:
- valkey_enable_ttl (bool, default false): feature flag.
- valkey_ttl_sec (int seconds, default 86400 when enabled but unset):
  per-key TTL; must be a positive integer.

_ThreadWorkerPool and ValkeyConnector accept ttl_seconds and, when set,
write keys with glide_sync.ExpirySet(SEC, ttl) (built lazily and cached;
the immutable ExpirySet is safe to share across worker threads). The
no-TTL path is unchanged. Adds docs and unit tests covering config
passthrough, defaulting, invalid-value rejection, and expiry application
on put.

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

Signed-off-by: Omer Rubinstein <omerrubi@amazon.com>

commits.author: nayeonikim
commits.message:
[Fix][RawBlock] Roll back put-task refs when dispatch scheduling fails (#3698)

batched_submit_put_task raises a ref on each pending object and reserves
its key in _put_tasks before handing the batch to the event loop. If
asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe fails (e.g. the loop is shutting down),
the raised refs and reserved keys for the unscheduled items leaked,
keeping the MemoryObj pinned and the key permanently blocked from future
puts. Wrap dispatch so the unscheduled items roll back their ref and
put-task entry; already-scheduled items still release theirs in the
coroutine's finally block.

Signed-off-by: Nayeon Kim <nayeoni.kim@samsung.com>

commits.author: nafis271
commits.message:
[GPU connector] Allow non-zero storage_offset in dim-0-padded layout (MTP + CPU offload) (#3853)

* [GPU connector] Allow non-zero storage_offset in dim-0-padded layout

The storage_offset == 0 constraint in _validate_dim0_padded_layout (added
in #3171, intentionally narrow) rejects MTP speculative decoding together
with LMCache CPU offload: with MTP on, per-layer KV are slice/narrow views
(storage_offset != 0). The offset is carried through on the GPU path --
CudaIPCWrapper round-trips storage_offset and get_group_data_ptrs passes
tensor.data_ptr() (offset baked in) to the transfer kernel, so
block_stride_elems is applied from the correct base. The CPU/SHM tier keeps
its own offset==0 assert.

Fixes #3821
